name: codex-project-governor
description: Codex Project Governor Harness v6.0.6: token-efficient docs navigation with DOCS_MANIFEST, section-level context retrieval, route-specific doc packs, session-learning memory ledgers, proactive memory-search startup policy, user-level Git install/update helper for local marketplaces, context-index v2, governed memory search, AGENTS.md rule-template drift migration, evidence gates, route guards, lifecycle hooks, GPT orchestration, and DESIGN.md UI workflows.

interface.shortDescription: Harness v6.0.6 for Codex project governance
interface.longDescription: Adds Harness v6.0.6: token-efficient docs navigation with DOCS_MANIFEST, section-level context retrieval, route-specific doc packs, and context budget gates, plus session-learning memory ledgers for failed commands and stale memories, proactive memory-search startup policy, one-command user-level Git install/update support for local marketplaces, one route source of truth, GPT-5.5 runtime planning, project-local state, evidence manifests, context-index v2, governed memory search, AGENTS.md rule-template drift migration, diff-derived route guard facts, lifecycle session start/end, stricter quality/merge readiness gates, doctor checks, and DESIGN.md-gated UI workflows with full-service or .env-design basic mode. The project carries durable state and auditable evidence while Codex executes, reviews, searches memory, compacts work, and avoids repeating known failures.
defaultPrompt: Use Project Governor Harness v6.0.6 to plan this change with DOCS_MANIFEST, section-level context retrieval, governed memory search, session learning, session state, evidence, route guard checks, and DESIGN.md UI gates when relevant.
Use Project Governor to initialize this existing repository without changing application code.
Use Project Governor to create a memory and iteration governance system for this project.
Use Project Governor to plan this change as an iteration, not a rewrite.
Use Project Governor to compact recent project activity into durable project memory.
Use Project Governor to advise whether project dependencies or tools should be upgraded before this change.
Use Project Governor to research candidate release versions before upgrade advice, including skipped versions, evidence quality, relevance, and risk.
Use Project Governor task-router as the single source of truth for route, risk score, confidence, guardrails, and evidence requirements.
Use Project Governor gpt55-auto-orchestrator to infer the workflow, context budget, model plan, subagents, session state, and quality gate automatically.
Use Project Governor context-indexer v2 to build DOCS_MANIFEST, query section-level project context, and avoid reading large initialization docs.
Use Project Governor session-lifecycle to start or end a task session and update .project-governor/state.
Use Project Governor evidence-manifest to map acceptance criteria, tests, review results, and docs-refresh decisions before merge readiness.
Use Project Governor route-guard with collect_diff_facts to verify the actual git diff still fits the selected route.
Use Project Governor harness-doctor to verify install shape, context freshness, state schemas, execution readiness, and evidence gates.
Use Project Governor clean-reinstall-manager to refresh project-owned governance without copying plugin-global assets.
Use Project Governor design-md-governor to lint, summarize, diff, or plan opt-in DESIGN.md governance before UI/style work.

Use Project Governor design-md-aesthetic-governor to require full-service Gemini/Stitch config or DESIGN_BASIC_MODE=1 from the shell environment or .env-design, DESIGN.md read proof, token discipline, aesthetic reference selection, and drift checks before UI/frontend edits.
